title: Informational Interviews and Reality Testing · Claim Audit: Action Feedback
summary: A candidate schedules a conversation to ask for a hidden referral, asks generic questions available online, records without consent, and treats one opinion as market truth. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the action feedback increment in consented role-reality interview study. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Close the loop by acting on one bounded insight, reporting the result when welcome, and revising the role hypothesis from combined evidence.
Separate observation, practitioner interpretation, candidate inference, contradiction, and unanswered question across multiple sources.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Action Feedback · Theory Action Feedback
steps.instruction:
theory case — A nominal learning conversation hides a referral request, asks generic questions, records without consent, and generalizes one practitioner view to a whole market. The theory variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Close the loop by acting on one bounded insight, reporting the result when welcome, and revising the role hypothesis from combined evidence.

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for close the loop by acting on one bounded insight, reporting the result when welcome, and revising the role hypothesis from combined evidence. For Predict Action Feedback · Theory Action Feedback, keep Action Feedback connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A nominal learning conversation hides a referral request, asks generic questions, records without consent, and generalizes one practitioner view to a whole market. The worked example variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Close the loop by acting on one bounded insight, reporting the result when welcome, and revising the role hypothesis from combined evidence. A defensible solution applies Action Feedback, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to consented role-reality interview study.

content.code:
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-5
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: The contact owes continuing mentorship after one conversation. This fails the Action Feedback contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook for Software Development and Quality Assurance (2024 data and 2024-2034 projections; page modified 2025-08-28) — Controls U.S.-specific occupation duties, collaboration expectations, important qualities, education norms, wage interpretation, and projection caveats.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Close the loop by acting on one bounded insight, reporting the result when welcome, and revising the role hypothesis from combined evidence.
